ab: Duuq maqtu ata pasaduu i laqbing mina’un tungquvali?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: Na maqtu, isaq ata pasaduu?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: Na maqtu aupa ihaan kaku ta? Na lasasing ata.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: Azoq munhaan ata u’ampukan ta, na nukan saak itu Bunun tu hulus.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: Mastaan manau’az naam asang tu dandan, saupa’a ti i maqatispung’in a dandan.
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: Na masmuav’in u’suii i, panhaanin mauq’na tu mintalapal.
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: Azoq ihaan’a kasu lus’an manaq tainga ta mun’i’ti.
ch: W: 那你射耳祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Ear Festival!

ab: Uu! Na pasaduu tudiipin.
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: Tusingav suu tu mavia asu tu musauqais haan asang ti i?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: Aupa asa saak ihaan asang ti masnava uvavaz’az.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: Viatu nitu sivng haan he’ci ta masnava i?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: Aupa i’iti naak a is’ang, duuq na haizang tamasaz’an saipuk anakanak tu taisis’aan.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: Maqansiap asu matin’un’i! Simaq a masnava suu?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: Sia inaaq qudas-binanauaz, siata a mastaan maqansiap matin’un laupaku ihaan inaam asang.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: Maaq suu i’liskinan i, na asa maqansiap matin’un?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: Maaq a matin’un’un a, isia Bunun tu sin’iqumis, maaki maqansiap amin a maikikingna matin’un a, na maqtu sia Bunun tu sin’iqumis sauqabasqabas.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: Mastaan masihal qudan a davus-maduq i.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: Anaq saak ka’davus qai!
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: Aii! Malmananu asu! Malbubuntu i ka’davus?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: Nii! Na muaz tastu-islungqu’an. Pu’ngaus’un’ang a dulaz siin tilas pit’a’li, pada’ngi’an i tamul, pisihalun mabizoq, na maqtu’in ladi’ngun haan qau’ngu.
ch: W:不用，大約一周就好了。把糯米和白米先蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
